description_long: This object is a vintage cathode ray tube (CRT) television with a large, curved glass screen set within a thick, black plastic bezel. The top and sides are encased in a woodgrain-patterned veneer, giving it a classic retro appearance. The front panel features a vertical array of physical control buttons and a speaker grille on the right side, with a small colored logo beneath the buttons. The back is constructed from dark, textured plastic with ventilation slats, molded warning labels, and a recessed panel likely for cable connections. The television stands on two short, sturdy feet, elevating it slightly above the ground. Its overall design is boxy and robust, typical of late 20th-century home electronics.
description: A vintage CRT television with a woodgrain exterior, large curved glass screen, and a row of control buttons on the front right side.
description_view_0: The front view shows the large curved screen, thick black bezel, woodgrain top, and a vertical row of control buttons with a speaker grille on the right.
description_view_1: The left side view highlights the woodgrain veneer and the depth of the television's body, with no visible controls or ports.
description_view_2: The rear view reveals a textured plastic back with ventilation slats, molded warning labels, and a recessed panel for cable connections.
description_view_3: The right side view mirrors the left, showing the woodgrain finish and the boxy profile of the television.
attribute_bezel_design: Thick black plastic bezel with a matte finish and rounded corners, framing the curved glass screen.
attribute_control_button_layout: Vertical row of physical buttons on the front right side, adjacent to the speaker grille.
